question_title: Which list will win the most seats in Bulgaria’s 2026 parliamentary election?
summary:
Bulgaria's April 19, 2026 parliamentary election is dominated by Progressive Bulgaria (PB), a brand-new coalition led by former President Rumen Radev. PB polls at 29-33% across all major Bulgarian pollsters (Trend, Sova Harris, Alpha Research, Gallup International, Myara, CAM, Market Links), with a massive 10-11 point lead over GERB-SDS at 19-21%. Since Progressive Bulgaria is not one of the named options, a PB victory resolves as "Other or tie." 

Threshold analysis shows PB would need to drop from ~31% to below ~20% for GERB to overtake them — an 11-point polling miss unprecedented in Bulgarian elections. Even under aggressive stress testing (30% chance of 3-10 point new-party overestimation), "Other or tie" wins 89% of Monte Carlo simulations. PP-DB (11-13%) and Revival (6-8%) are distant third and fifth respectively, with negligible probability of winning the most seats.

factors.description: Progressive Bulgaria (Radev's new coalition) leads all polls at 29-33%, well ahead of GERB-SDS at 19-21%. Seven independent pollsters confirm this lead. PB is NOT one of the named options, so a PB win = 'Other or tie'.
The polling lead (~10-11 percentage points) is massive. PB needs to drop from 31% to below 20% for GERB to overtake — an 11-point polling miss with no precedent in Bulgarian elections.

Progressive Bulgaria is a brand-new party (formed March 2, 2026). New parties carry higher uncertainty — actual vote share could underperform polls due to untested organization or voter enthusiasm gap.
GERB-SDS has won the most seats in every Bulgarian election since 2021 (6 consecutive first-place finishes). Strong party machine and organizational infrastructure.
Election is ~4 weeks away. Campaign just started March 20. Time exists for events to shift the race, though a 10+ point swing would be extraordinary.
Radev is a well-known former president with high personal popularity. This mitigates typical new-party risk — the coalition has a recognized leader and experienced partners.
PP-DB polls at 11-13%, Revival at 6-8%. Neither is remotely close to first place. Probability of either winning most seats is negligible.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Bulgaria's 2026 parliamentary election (April 19)** is being reshaped by a new entrant: **Progressive Bulgaria**, a coalition led by former President Rumen Radev who resigned from the presidency in January 2026.

### Key Finding
Progressive Bulgaria is **not one of the named options**, so its widely-expected victory resolves as **"Other or tie"**.

### Polling Consensus (March 2026, 7+ independent pollsters):
| Party | Vote Share | Projected Seats |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|
| **Progressive Bulgaria** | 29-33% | ~85 seats |
| GERB-SDS | 19-21% | ~65 seats |
| PP-DB | 11-13% | ~35 seats |
| DPS | 9.5-11% | ~32 seats |
| Revival | 6-8% | ~23 seats |

### Probabilities
- **Other or tie: 86%** — Progressive Bulgaria wins most seats
- **GERB-SDS: 12%** — requires unprecedented ~11-point polling miss
- **PP-DB: 1%** — distant third, negligible chance
- **Revival: 1%** — distant fifth, negligible chance

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option whose list wins the most seats in Bulgaria’s 2026 parliamentary election before April 25, 2026, using the Wikipedia page for the election at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Bulgarian_parliamentary_election. This question resolves to the tie-or-other option if none of the named lists wins the most seats outright or if there is a tie for most seats.
fine_print: Use final nationwide seat totals. If the Wikipedia page is unavailable, incomplete, or internally inconsistent by the resolution time, use Bulgaria’s Central Election Commission results. Resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: Bulgaria Central Election Commission https://www.cik.bg/ ; official results portal https://results.cik.bg/
